display_note: This value comes from an official literacy-rate series and appears to rely on national reporting rather than independent skills assessment. Independent verification is limited, and the latest staged observation is old (2008). Treat this as a weak-confidence published figure, not a directly verified adult-skills estimate.

This near-universal literacy figure is plausible, but it is an administrative/statistical literacy-rate observation, not a direct adult-skills proficiency result. High literacy-rate values can coexist with weaker performance on richer literacy assessments, so avoid reading it as proof of uniformly strong adult reading proficiency.
